'''Beck-tori''' were [[Wikipedia:aquatic|aquatic]] [[Parasite/Legends|parasites]] from [[Nam Priax]]'s warm seas that grew to extreme sizes. Because the movement of a potential host through the water attracted Beck-tori, they sometimes would mistakenly attack boats and submersible craft. They were known to have fought frequently with the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. The beck-tori had a strong connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], which they could use to heal themselves.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Beck-tori.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Gungans battling a beck-tori]]
The creature resembled a flat, elongated [[Leech/Legends|leech]]. It had rows of scraping spines along the underside of its head that led to a gaping maw. After using its spines to attach to a living victim, a Beck-tori would flex its body to grind, dislodge, and consume flesh. The creature usually attached to a large aquatic predator before swallowing parts of it. Eventually, the Beck-tori would grind its way into the victim's body cavity, exposing its host to other invasive organisms. The host's body provided a medium in which the Beck-tori could lay its eggs before moving on to new prey.

Beck-tori larvae were translucent creatures only a few centimeters long; they typically posed little danger to anything larger than a normal fish. At their average length, their main weapons were their abrasive spikes, although full-grown specimen were capable of swallowing their prey whole. Adult Beck-tori could breathe both air and water.

The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] took steps to ensure that the beck-tori were never transplanted to other worlds, but were unable to prevent several infestations. Most notably, an outbreak on [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] took more than fifty years to eradicate.<ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> The creatures have also been spotted on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].
